channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police investigate antisemitic arson attack on Jewish community ambulances in north London
item.description: The Metropolitan Police are investigating a suspected arson attack on four ambulances belonging to the Jewish Community Ambulance service, Hatzolah, in the north London area of Golders Green. The inci...

item.title: Parliament blocks Sh280m proposal for Musalia Mudavadi's office renovations over cost concerns
item.description: The Daily Nation reports that Members of Parliament have blocked a proposal to spend Sh280 million to upgrade the office of Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi. The move comes amid broader concer...

item.title: Pakistan commemorates Pakistan Day with simple ceremonies under austerity plan amid Gulf oil crisis
item.description: Pakistan marked Pakistan Day on March 23, 2026, with patriotic spirit but low-key observances due to government-imposed austerity measures triggered by the global oil crisis following the US-Israel wa...

item.title: Security operatives foil armed attack on Kwara church, rescue three worshippers
item.description: On Sunday, joint security operatives repelled a suspected bandits’ attack on an Evangelical Church Winning All (ECWA) in the Omugo community, Ifelodun Local Government Area of Kwara State. The attacke...

item.title: Slash enjoys a low-key supermarket visit with his longtime partner
item.description: Guns N' Roses guitarist Slash, born Saul Hudson, was recently seen on a low-profile supermarket outing with his longtime partner Meegan Hodges. The 60-year-old rock star traded his iconic guitar for a...

item.title: OpenAI launches advertising program for free and Go ChatGPT users in the US
item.description: OpenAI is preparing to display advertisements on its free and Go versions of ChatGPT in the United States, marking a major step in its attempt to diversify revenue streams. The move was confirmed by a...

item.title: Tony Elumelu Foundation selects 3,200 young African entrepreneurs for 2026 support programme
item.description: The Tony Elumelu Foundation is set to announce the 3,200 beneficiaries for its 2026 cohort of the flagship entrepreneurship programme. This marks the 12th year of the initiative that supports early-st...

item.title: Key considerations before switching to an electric vehicle in Australia
item.description: As petrol prices continue to climb due to international instability, more Australians are eyeing electric vehicles (EVs) as a practical way to lower fuel costs and emissions. The article explains the ...

item.title: Top High-Tech Cleaning Gadgets Worth the Investment in 2026
item.description: The market for cleaning tech is flooded with products that promise convenience but often fail to deliver. In this article, we highlight five cleaning gadgets that truly make a difference, with product...

item.title: India’s Struggle for the Right to Die with Dignity: Aruna Shanbaug to Harish Rana
item.description: India’s legal battle over the right to die with dignity has progressed over decades, with two landmark cases leading the way. The 2011 Aruna Shanbaug case, after the nurse's long coma due to a brutal ...

item.title: Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam fields full slate of 30 candidates for Puducherry Assembly polls
item.description: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), the political outfit founded by Tamil actor Vijay, has decided to go it alone in the forthcoming Puducherry Assembly elections. The party announced a complete list of c...

item.title: The Danger of the Anti-Dynasty Bill: A Deceptive Hope for Change
item.description: The editorial discusses how the passage of a diluted anti-dynasty bill under the Marcos administration could further entrench political dynasties in the Philippines. The bill prohibits family members ...

item.title: Bungoma chief speaks out against gender‑based violence, land disputes and community silence
item.description: The article tells the story of a senior traditional leader in Bungoma County who has become a key figure in resisting gender‑based violence, land conflicts and the culture of silence that often shield...

item.title: Business Council Cautions Against Mass Office Exodus Over Fuel Shortages
item.description: The Business Council of Australia has warned against a nationwide move to working from home as a means of conserving fuel amidst the global oil crisis. While acknowledging that some workers may need t...

item.title: ICE Agents to Assist TSA with Basic Duties During U.S. Airport Security Delays
item.description: In response to long airport security delays caused by a partial shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents will assist the Transportation Securit...

item.title: Algoma Steel begins layoffs affecting hundreds in Sault Ste. Marie
item.description: The city of Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, is facing a difficult period as Algoma Steel begins implementing major layoffs. About 300 employees are being let go, a move that community leaders link partly t...

item.title: Understanding Grooming Tactics through the Epstein Case
item.description: In this episode of *Today in Focus*, journalist and author Lucia Osborne-Crowley, known for her book *The Lasting Harm*, joins Annie Kelly to discuss the lessons society should learn from the crimes o...

item.title: Durban North: Couple Found Dead in Apparent Murder-Suicide Incident
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded in Glenashley, Durban North, on Saturday, leaving two people dead in what police are investigating as a suspected murder-suicide. According to authorities, a man allegedly s...

item.title: Oyo PDP Aspirants Engage Advisory Council Ahead of 2027 Governorship Election
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, don give his advisory council the work to meet and reason with all credible aspirants wey dey contest under the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) for the 2027 governorsh...

item.title: The U.S. Unilateral Trade Tools and Their Impact on Global Trade Agreements
item.description: The U.S. has historically used Section 301 of its Trade Act to unilaterally impose tariffs on countries it deems to be violating trade agreements or engaging in unfair practices. This tool has been pa...
